title: coreopsis rhyacophila
intro: Coreopsis rhyacophila, also known as the mountain tickseed, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of western North America, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Coreopsis rhyacophila are believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, reduction of inflammation, and antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
